insight1: Rentals soar to circa 100 between the hours of 0:00 and 6:00 and between the hours of 17:00 to 19:00 but at totally different temperatures of circa -100 in the morning slot and +20 in the early evening slot.
insight2: There is a huge difference, polar opposites infact, on the effect that temperature has during 10:00-15:00 on a workday in particular,people must have free time to walk if they choose during hotter temperatures.
insight3: the negative correlations between wind speed and rentals completely evaporates after a wind speed of 0.7.
insight4: 25 degrees is the optimal time to get high rentals, not only does it climb rapidly as it approaches that temperature but there is a steady decline after that time, people feel too hot and fatigued at 26 degrees onwards.
insight5: there is a surge of rentals between 7:50 and 8:50 and also between 16:45 and 18:45 - it is no coincidence that this is the most common time people arrived at and leave work on a workday which happens to also be backed up on the graph which shows workdays produce more rentals - as non workdays would not benefit from these two spikes in rentals.

insight1: Rentals increase steadily over the week from a low on Sunday and reaching a peak on Saturday when almost 3 times as many rentals are made compare to the next most popular day, Friday.
insight2: Increasing windspeed produces a negative effect on rentals, with an almost linear effect between 045 and 0.65 windspeed. However at the extremes of the range of windspeed, below 0.43 and above 0.7 windspeed changes have no effect.
insight3: Rentals generally increase as temperature increases up to a tmeperature of 25 degrees when rentals start to fall as the temperature increases. This is however time dependent, with temperature effects following this pattern from 10 am in the morning. From midnight until 6am the temperature effect is reversed.
insight4: Rentals generally peak between 7.30am and 8.30am and then again between 16.30 and 18.30 in the afternoon. However this effect is seen from Monday to Thursday, with Friday, Saturday and Sunday showing peak rentals between 9am and 5pm with this effect being strongest on Saturday.
insight5: Generally very few rentals are made during the hours of midnight to 5am, except for on Sunday mornings, and when the weather is below zero degrees, this is by far the most popular rental time, with more than double the rentals during this time than later in the day.

insight1: Given the data in the first table, it is shown that the highest rates of rentals is at half past 16:00. This means that most people are renting bikes after work.
insight2: While the data in the first table shows that the highest rates of rentals starts at 16:30, the second table shows that the rates are even higher when the temperature is at a nice 24-26 degrees. So that proves that on a day with a temperature of 25 degrees at 16:30, this would have a great impact on rentals.
insight3: In the third table, it is shown that rentals start to decrease when the windspeed is at about 0.44, and a slight increase on rentals when the windspeed is less than that. So on a cold, windy day, rentals should be expected to decrease.
insight4: It is evident, based on the Weekday and Workday tables that more people rent bikes on a Saturday, and on the days they work. So, given that data, it is safe to assume that people will rent more on Saturdays that they work.
insight5: On a work day, the best time for an increase in bike rentals is around 8:00, and on a non-work day the best time for an increase in bike rentals is starting at 11:00 with another slight increase in rentals around 15:00. Given all of this information, and the information in other insights, the biggest increase in bike rentals would be on a non-working Saturday, at around 15:00 with a temperature of 25 degrees.

insight1: Windspeed matters little for this venture.
insight3: Rentals are highest on Fridays and Saturdays, but much lower Sundays and Mondays.
insight4: Workdays, on average, have a negative effect on rentals.
insight5: Rentals are fairly consistent from morning to evening, with only a slight variance up and then down in the early and late evening.

insight2: On a weekday Early morning and early evening are the busier times
